sections.paragraph:
f. (from the root אָוַה No. I)-

(1) desire, longing, whether good and just, Psa 10:17 21:3 or wicked, Psa 112:10.

(2) in a bad sense, lust, desire (Luft, Gelüft). Num 11:4, הִתְאַוּוּ תַּאֲוָה “they lusted a lust.” Psal. 78:29 , 30 Psa 78:30. קִבְרֹות הַתַּאֲוָה the graves of lust, Num 11:34, 35 Num 11:35.

(3) delight, object of desire. מַאֲכַל תַּאְוָה food of delight, i.e. delicate, Job 33:20 Gen 3:6 also, honour, ornament, Gen 49:26 Pro 19:22.
